checks.finding: Every AC-NNN referenced in a task Requirements field (AC-001 through AC-017, spread across T-001..T-011) resolves to a real row in acceptance-tests.md's criteria table. No dangling AC ID found.

All 11 tasks (T-001..T-011) carry a '### Blockers' section with either the literal 'None' (T-001, T-003, T-006) or a comma-separated T-NNN list (e.g. T-002: 'T-001'; T-010: 'T-002, T-003, T-004, T-005, T-006, T-007, T-008, T-009, T-011'). No range notation or prose found. Matches precheck's blockers_format_valid: true.
REQ-001 through REQ-011 (all requirements in requirements.md) each appear in at least one task's Requirements field in tasks.md (verified via per-ID grep count, all >=2).
AC-001 through AC-017 (all criteria in acceptance-tests.md) each appear in at least one task's Requirements field or Done-When mapping line in tasks.md (verified via per-ID grep count, all >=2).
Every task T-001..T-011 has a non-empty Requirements: field citing at least one REQ-NNN (e.g. T-001: 'REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-009, AC-003'; T-009: 'REQ-010, AC-012'). No orphan tasks found.

No task title contains 'test', 'spec', or 'verify' (case-insensitive), and no task's scope is exclusively test-file changes -- each task (e.g. T-001, T-005, T-008) pairs implementation files with its own test files under one Requirements-linked scope. No orphaned test task identified.

All 11 tasks declare 'Approval: Draft' and 'Status: Planned'. This is attempt 2 round 2, so the rule requires Approval != Approved and Status in {Planned, In Progress, Blocked}; Draft/Planned satisfies both conditions.

All 11 tasks declare both Risk: and Required Workflow:, and every pairing matches the risk-gate-matrix: low->test-after (T-001, T-009, T-010), medium->acceptance-first (T-002, T-003, T-004, T-007, T-008), high->tdd (T-005, T-006, T-011). precheck-result.json records workflow_match_precheck: PASS, consistent with this manual verification.

AC-004 is referenced by both T-002 and T-003 with distinct verification scopes: T-002 verifies text/path/URL seed intake plus error-path fixtures via tests/sdd-domain/artifact-set.Tests.ps1, while T-003 verifies reverse-mode consumption of investigation.md via tests/sdd-domain/reverse-seed.Tests.ps1 (a separate clause of AC-004). AC-001 is referenced by both T-004 and T-010: T-004 verifies the new skill's visibility via tests/hooks/domain-model-visibility.Tests.ps1 (implementation-level check), while T-010 verifies the repository-wide expectation via tests/validate-repository.ps1, which acceptance-tests.md's TEST-001 names as the canonical Test Target for AC-001. Both pairs have differentiated, explicitly named test scopes per the calibration allowance for shared AC references with distinct scopes. Re-checked the specific prior-round AC-011 finding: T-001's Requirements field now reads 'REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-009, AC-003' and its Done-When traceability line cites only that same set -- AC-011 has been fully removed from T-001, leaving T-010 as sole owner. Fix confirmed.

Every Blockers reference (T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004, T-005, T-006, T-007, T-008, T-009, T-011) resolves to a task ID present in dependency-graph.json's nodes array (['T-001'..'T-011']). No dangling task reference found.
BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS, so this check runs. Traced all 20 edges in dependency-graph.json: a valid topological order exists with no node revisited. No cycle detected.
Titles using 'and' (T-001, T-005, T-007) each connect tightly coupled, mandatory housekeeping or single-thread infrastructure concerns. No task title or scope connects two decoupled feature concerns.

Every task's Done When list includes at least one concrete, inspectable item beyond the standard boilerplate. Re-checked the specific prior-round T-001 finding: its Done-When now names the specific test file (tests/sdd-domain/contract-schema.Tests.ps1) and its exact fixture-level pass/reject outcomes, replacing the prior generic-boilerplate-only list. Fix confirmed; no vague verbs found in any Done When item repo-wide.

All 11 task IDs (T-001..T-011) appear in traceability.md's Requirement/Design/Code Target table. All requirement IDs exist in requirements.md. Layer-spec anchors confirmed present as real headings in the respective hash-bound layer documents. No dangling references in either direction.
